stories.title: Next.js project scaffold with Firebase & Tailwind
acceptanceCriteria: Next.js 14 project exists at /Users/jcsong/codework/MeetingSummary with App Router
package.json includes firebase, openai, tailwindcss dependencies
tailwind.config.ts and postcss.config.js exist
src/lib/firebase.ts exports initialized Firebase app, auth, firestore, storage
src/types/meeting.ts defines Meeting, ActionPoint, STTProviderName types
TypeScript compiles with no errors (npx tsc --noEmit)

stories.title: Firebase Auth — login/logout with Google
acceptanceCriteria: src/lib/auth.ts exports signInWithGoogle and signOut helpers
src/contexts/AuthContext.tsx provides user state via React context
src/app/login/page.tsx renders a Google sign-in button
src/middleware.ts redirects unauthenticated users to /login
Firestore security rules file (firestore.rules) exists with user-scoped read/write

stories.title: STT pluggable adapter + Whisper implementation
acceptanceCriteria: src/lib/stt/types.ts defines STTProvider interface with transcribe(file: File): Promise<string>
src/lib/stt/whisper.ts implements WhisperProvider using OpenAI Whisper API (model: whisper-1)
src/lib/stt/index.ts exports getSTTProvider(name: STTProviderName) factory function
src/app/api/transcribe/route.ts accepts multipart/form-data with audio file and provider param, returns {transcript: string}
API route handles mp3/mp4/wav/m4a file types

stories.title: AI Processing — GPT-4o summary + action points extraction
acceptanceCriteria: src/app/api/process/route.ts accepts {transcript: string} and returns {summary: string, actionPoints: ActionPoint[]}
GPT-4o prompt instructs: summary in Korean prose paragraphs followed by bullet points
GPT-4o returns structured action points with assignee, task, dueDate (optional), priority (high/medium/low) fields
Response is parsed and validated before returning to client
API uses gpt-4o model with response_format JSON mode or structured output

stories.title: Meeting creation UI — text input or audio upload
acceptanceCriteria: src/app/meetings/new/page.tsx renders two input modes: text paste tab and audio upload tab
Text mode: textarea for pasting meeting transcript
Audio mode: file input accepting .mp3/.mp4/.wav/.m4a with STT provider dropdown selector
STT provider dropdown shows at least: Whisper (default) option
Submit triggers transcription (audio mode) then AI processing, shows loading state
On success, saves meeting to Firestore under users/{uid}/meetings/{id} and redirects to meeting detail

stories.title: Meeting detail page — view, inline-edit summary and action points
acceptanceCriteria: src/app/meetings/[id]/page.tsx fetches meeting from Firestore and displays title, transcript, summary, action points
Summary section has an Edit button that switches to a textarea for inline editing; Save persists to Firestore
Action points section shows cards with all 4 fields (assignee, task, dueDate, priority)
Each action point card has Edit and Delete buttons
Add New Action Point button opens inline form with all 4 fields
All edits persist to Firestore immediately on save

stories.title: Meeting list page — chronological meeting history
acceptanceCriteria: src/app/meetings/page.tsx fetches and displays all meetings for the authenticated user
Meetings are sorted by createdAt descending (newest first)
Each meeting card shows: title, createdAt date, number of action points
Clicking a meeting card navigates to /meetings/[id]
Empty state shown when no meetings exist
New Meeting button links to /meetings/new

stories.title: Firebase Storage for audio files
acceptanceCriteria: Audio files are uploaded to Firebase Storage at path users/{uid}/audio/{meetingId}.{ext} before STT processing
Meeting document in Firestore stores audioStoragePath field pointing to the storage path
Meeting detail page shows a link or audio player if audioStoragePath exists

stories.title: Environment config & deployment readiness
acceptanceCriteria: .env.local.example documents all required env vars: NEXT_PUBLIC_FIREBASE_*, OPENAI_API_KEY
next.config.ts exists and is valid
vercel.json or README documents deployment steps
npm run build completes without errors
No hardcoded API keys in source files
